formInstance.inputDocument:
Charlotte Miller is a distinguished member of the Art History Department, known for her expertise in Renaissance and classical art. She is applying to present at the conference under the Topic Area of Art History with her Presentation Title, "Classical Influence on Renaissance Art: A Comparative Analysis." In her Presentation Abstract, Charlotte outlines her plan to explore the influence of classical Greek and Roman art on Renaissance artists. The presentation will involve comparing key works of Renaissance art with their classical predecessors, focusing on the ways the Renaissance rediscovered and reinterpreted classical forms. Attendees will be able to achieve the following Learning Objectives: 1. Learn how classical art influenced the Renaissance. 2. Understand the key characteristics of Renaissance art. 3. Explore the connection between classical and Renaissance aesthetics.

Charlotte Miller's Professional Biography details her tenure as an art historian, during which she has authored several publications on the relationship between ancient and Renaissance art and taught art history for over 15 years. Her Previous Speaking Experience includes being a Speaker at the Renaissance Art Symposium 2023 and a Panelist at the Classical Art Influence Conference 2022. For her Lecture presentation, she will require Special Technical Requirements, including a projector and sound system, to ensure the effective delivery of her content. Charlotte has expressed her agreement with the speaker guidelines and terms of participation, demonstrating her commitment to contributing her expertise at the conference. For any further correspondence, she can be reached at charlotte.miller82@outlook.com or by phone at 567-890-2345.

goldAnswers.Presentation Title: Classical Influence on Renaissance Art: A Comparative Analysis
goldAnswers.Presentation Abstract: This presentation will explore the influence of classical Greek and Roman art on Renaissance artists. We will compare key works of Renaissance art with their classical predecessors, analyzing the ways in which the Renaissance rediscovered and reinterpreted classical forms.
goldAnswers.Learning Objectives: 1. Learn how classical art influenced the Renaissance. 2. Understand the key characteristics of Renaissance art. 3. Explore the connection between classical and Renaissance aesthetics.
goldAnswers.Professional Biography: Charlotte Miller is an art historian specializing in Renaissance and classical art. She has authored several publications on the relationship between ancient and Renaissance art and has taught art history for over 15 years.
goldAnswers.Previous Speaking Experience: 1. Speaker at Renaissance Art Symposium 2023. 2. Panelist at Classical Art Influence Conference 2022.
